div1.entryFree:
Chaps, never used in the sing.; 1) the jaws, the mouth: open your c. again, Tp. II, 2, 89. now doth Death line his dead c. with steel, John II, 352. I'll thrust my knife in your mouldy c. H4B II, 4, 139. before his c. be stained with crimson blood, H6B III, 1, 259. he unseamed him from the nave to the c. Mcb. I, 2, 22. then, world, thou hast a pair of c., no more, Ant. III, 5, 14.
2) wrinkles: my frosty signs and c. of age, Tit. V, 3, 77. In Lucr. 1452 M. Edd., according to Q7, chaps; all the other Qq chops, q. v.
